edge_type_library.definition: An artifact or pattern belongs to an analytic asset plane.
A pattern could support a bounded inference about an asset plane.
A counterfactual pattern could be associated with a stage; it does not assert actual collection.
A limitation blocks an evidentiary leap from the source node.
A stage may emit a professional-judgment signal.
A stage may emit a process or workflow signal.
A workflow stage can normally lead to another stage; this is not a mandatory path.
A stage creates or maintains an artifact class.
A defensive control limits a stage or counterfactual pattern.
A counterfactual pattern values a signal class for the stated hypothetical objective.
A public source supports or materially constrains the modeled statement.

nodes.label: Token, latency, cost, and recurrence metadata
Accept, reject, regenerate, edit, and override feedback
Exception rationale, debrief, and outcome loop
Public docket, opinion, and deal-document reconstruction
Support, feedback, bug-report, and shared-conversation channel
Co-development, managed service, and integration partnership
Vertical integration, investment, or acquisition governance scenario
Keep trust and payment rails outside the model boundary
convert client instructions into enforceable matter policy
exclude credentials and payment rails from generative AI
prevent communications from becoming provider telemetry
separate compliance facts from strategic rationale
ban secondary use and cross-client derivation
separate public-corpus research from matter context
Prohibit raw-source retention outside the firm
separate public artifact from private genealogy
Preserve source genealogy and reuse authority
separate workforce analytics from model improvement
prohibit invoice text training or secondary use
never send raw narratives by default
Market intelligence, business development, and referrals
nodes.scope: Prospects, panels, referrals, pitches, relationship ownership, rate proposals, and market positioning before a specific engagement.

nodes.label: Prospective-client contact and pre-intake
nodes.scope: Initial contact, problem summary, adverse parties, urgency, jurisdiction, and routing before a representation is accepted.

nodes.label: Conflicts and eligibility clearance
nodes.scope: Entity and affiliate matching, adversity analysis, waivers, screens, ethical walls, and clearance decisions.

nodes.label: Client acceptance and enterprise risk
nodes.scope: Accept-or-decline decisions, competence and capacity checks, sanctions or KYC review, credit, insurer, reputational, and strategic fit.

nodes.label: Engagement, scope, pricing, and client instructions
nodes.scope: Engagement terms, scope and exclusions, outside-counsel rules, budgets, fees, assumptions, and client instructions including approved AI use.

nodes.label: Matter opening and information governance
nodes.scope: Matter identity, taxonomy, repositories, team, residency, retention, legal holds, access groups, ethical walls, and connector policies.

nodes.label: Staffing, resourcing, budget, and workplan
nodes.scope: Work breakdown, roles, skills, staffing pyramid, supervision, timeline, workload, budget, and internal handoffs.

nodes.label: Fact development, evidence, diligence, and discovery
nodes.scope: Chronologies, custodians, evidence graphs, collections, diligence and discovery plans, review tags, interviews, and investigations.

nodes.label: Legal research and analysis
nodes.scope: Issue trees, authorities, citator status, jurisdictional rules, research queries, memoranda, analogies, counterarguments, and uncertainty.

nodes.label: Strategy, options, exposure, and matter economics
nodes.scope: Goals, scenario trees, alternatives, risk assessments, settlement or deal ranges, expected reactions, exposure, and strategic plans.

nodes.label: Drafting, review, and redlining
nodes.scope: Drafts, comments, diffs, clause or argument libraries, fallbacks, approvals, and iterative review of legal work product.

nodes.label: Negotiation, advocacy, filings, hearings, and transaction execution
nodes.scope: Offers, counteroffers, preparation, depositions, filings, hearings, negotiation moves, closing checklists, and execution steps.

nodes.label: Supervision, quality assurance, privilege review, ethics, and candor
nodes.scope: Review assignments, signoffs, citation and fact checks, privilege review, validation, ethical escalation, overrides, and incident handling.

nodes.label: Client communication, advice, instruction, and decision
nodes.scope: Status, advice, options, consent, client instructions, recommendations, decisions, and secure communication throughout the matter.

nodes.label: Resolution, settlement, judgment, closing, and remedies
nodes.scope: Settlement, judgment, remedies, closing conditions, executed documents, closing books, obligation schedules, and residual risk.

nodes.label: Timekeeping, work in progress, and pricing variance
nodes.scope: Time entries, narratives, task codes, write-downs, budgets, variance, profitability, rework, and task-level economics.

nodes.label: Billing and e-billing
nodes.scope: Invoices, billing narratives, task codes, outside-counsel-rule compliance, rejections, disputes, write-offs, and adjustments.

nodes.label: Collections, cash application, trust accounting, and disbursement
nodes.scope: Accounts receivable, payment promises, receipts, remittances, fee disputes, client trust ledgers, disbursements, and cash application.

nodes.label: Matter closure, retention, return, destruction, and relationship follow-up
nodes.scope: Closing memos, final disposition, return or destruction, legal holds, access deprovisioning, conflict updates, client surveys, and follow-up.

nodes.label: Knowledge capture, precedent, debrief, and reuse
nodes.scope: Playbooks, clause and argument banks, checklists, taxonomies, deviations, rationales, debriefs, lessons, precedents, and reuse governance.

nodes.label: Talent, training, apprenticeship, evaluation, and succession
nodes.scope: Competency maps, assignments, review comments, training, mentoring, performance, promotion, succession, and communities of expertise.

nodes.label: Vendors, technology, data, AI, cybersecurity, and records
nodes.scope: Vendor inventory, data flows, model and connector selection, retention and deletion, security assessments, incidents, audits, and portability.

nodes.label: Firm governance, professional independence, quality, risk, and economics
nodes.scope: Policies, committees, compensation, capital allocation, practice strategy, insurance, quality, ownership, independence, and enterprise risk.

nodes.label: debrief, exception rationale, and outcome linkage
firm risk appetite and strategic plan
That a provider plans an acquisition
A contract promise does not prove runtime behavior, and service processing does not prove training or secondary use.
The content of tool parameters or results
A general engagement clause does not prove informed consent for every later tool, data class, or purpose.
Cross-customer learning or intent to replace a firm
Why an edit occurred without context
Payment timing or an aging category does not prove client solvency, satisfaction, or the merits of a fee dispute.
Model training, retention, or cross-customer use
That any ownership form is lawful in a jurisdiction
Whether formal rules match actual behavior
A name match does not prove a conflict, and a cleared matter does not prove the absence of every conflict.
That metadata is used for model training or product discovery
An override or error does not by itself establish negligence, incompetence, intent, or systemic policy.
A stable attorney or firm preference
Legal complexity, quality, or value from time alone
A modeled scenario does not prove intent, legal merit, authority to settle, or the eventual course of action.
A matter taxonomy label does not establish the client's full objectives or the controlling legal analysis.
Provider retention, aggregation, or hostile intent
Why a public position was selected
Confidential advice, discarded alternatives, settlement logic, or client objectives
An outcome does not prove causation, strategy quality, client satisfaction, or the hidden alternatives considered.
A research query does not prove the firm's position, the client's objective, or the final advice.
Ownership, investment, partnership, or acquisition does not imply authority to access client information, waive privilege, or direct legal judgment.
Hostile intent from the existence of an optional feedback channel
A review tag does not prove admissibility, privilege, credibility, or the truth of a fact.
A public filing or executed term does not reveal confidential advice, rejected alternatives, internal disagreements, or full client objectives.
That similar behavior is proprietary rather than client-, regulator-, court-, or vendor-driven
An edit does not prove why it was made, whether it is correct, or whether it represents general firm policy.
A billing rejection does not prove that work lacked value, was unnecessary, or was performed incorrectly.
A decline category does not establish the full reason, legal merit, or a firm's general policy.
Reason for the decision without authorized context
Meaning or correctness of the work
A closed docket or matter status does not prove complete deletion, resolved risk, or client authorization for reuse.
An assignment does not prove expertise, performance, authorship, or responsibility for the final result.
A staffing or review graph does not prove individual competence, future performance, or ownership of institutional knowledge.
Whether a rule is firm-authored or client-required
Authority to train or improve a provider model
Any provider's intent to generalize the material
That observed topology is firm-designed rather than vendor-default
A pitch theme does not prove the firm's internal legal strategy or a provider's intent.
An initial contact does not prove that a representation formed or that the preliminary classification was correct.
Why a named decision was made
Communication metadata does not prove the substance, adequacy, or acceptance of legal advice.
Privilege waiver, client consent, or authority to reuse information
Time, tokens, or cost do not prove legal complexity, work quality, value, or substantive strategy.
A repeated deviation does not prove a general rule, causal success, provider retention, or authority for cross-client reuse.
Correctness or universality of the declared policy

nodes.boundary: Information relating to a representation is broader than attorney-client privilege; privilege, work product, confidentiality, privacy, and trade secret rules remain distinct.
nodes.definition: Identities, facts, communications, evidence, drafts, objectives, advice, personal data, financial information, and prospective-client information.
nodes.label: Client and matter content

nodes.boundary: Ownership or partnership never implies unrestricted access, privilege waiver, secondary-use authority, or permission to direct professional judgment.
nodes.definition: Ownership, professional independence, policy authority, vendor rights, system custody, retention, deletion, audit, pricing, and capital-allocation decisions.
nodes.label: Economic and governance control plane

nodes.boundary: This is an operational representation of judgment, not a copy of a lawyer's mind and not proof that a choice was legally correct.
nodes.definition: Issue framing, choices, risk weighting, fallbacks, escalation thresholds, edits, rationales, negotiation posture, and quality standards.
nodes.label: Professional judgment ontology

nodes.boundary: Process structure can be sensitive even when no document text is present; metadata combinations can identify matters or institutional priorities.
nodes.definition: Workflow states, roles, queues, systems, task taxonomies, templates, approvals, economics, exceptions, and handoffs.
nodes.label: Operational and process ontology

nodes.boundary: Public outputs show only selected visible conduct and cannot reveal sealed material, unfiled work, confidential settlements, abandoned strategies, or internal deliberation.
nodes.definition: Dockets, filed documents, opinions, public deal and regulatory documents, biographies, and authorized public statements.
nodes.label: Public-derived legal evidence
anthropics/claude-for-legal at pinned commit
nodes.limitation: Public plugin, prompt, connector, and orchestration layer only; closed Anthropic systems are absent.

nodes.label: Commercial Terms of Service
nodes.limitation: Public standard terms bind only the retrieved text. They do not establish that a particular firm is covered by these terms, its order form or negotiated terms, actual configuration, data flow, access, enforcement, or third-party handling.
nodes.locator: Captured HTML: Sections A.2, B, and E covering third-party features, Customer Content rights and model training terms, and confidentiality

nodes.label: Is my data used for model training?
nodes.limitation: A captured rendering and a secondary observation disagreed on feedback retention duration; reverify before publication.
nodes.locator: Commercial default, feedback, retention, and admin controls

nodes.label: API and data retention
nodes.limitation: Feature-specific vendor documentation may change. It states documented policy/eligibility conditions, not a particular legal customer's configuration, actual retention, personnel access, data flow, or model-training use.
nodes.locator: Captured HTML: API and data retention sections, feature eligibility table, stateful products, and ZDR exclusions

nodes.label: Freshfields and Anthropic team up to co-build AI legal workflows
nodes.limitation: Evidence of consensual collaboration, not covert reuse.
nodes.locator: Firm-wide co-development, deployment, and connection to institutional knowledge

nodes.label: ABA Model Rule 1.6: Confidentiality of Information
nodes.limitation: The Model Rules are models, not jurisdiction-specific law; adoption and interpretation vary.

nodes.label: ABA Formal Opinion 512: Generative Artificial Intelligence Tools
nodes.limitation: General ethics guidance under the Model Rules; fact-specific and not jurisdiction-specific legal advice.
nodes.locator: Sections on competence, confidentiality, communication, supervision, candor, and fees
nodes.publisher: American Bar Association Standing Committee on Ethics and Professional Responsibility

nodes.label: ABA Model Rule 5.4: Professional Independence of a Lawyer
nodes.limitation: Ownership and fee-sharing rules vary by jurisdiction; this model rule cannot support a nationwide categorical conclusion.

nodes.label: Find a Case (PACER)
nodes.limitation: PACER coverage does not include every state matter, every historical record, sealed material, unfiled work, or internal deliberation.
nodes.locator: Electronic case files, dockets, opinions, historical records, and access terms
nodes.publisher: Administrative Office of the U.S. Courts

nodes.label: What information is available through PACER?
nodes.limitation: Public availability does not make the record complete, representative, or a source of internal strategy.
nodes.locator: Public case and docket information plus stated exclusions for sealed and restricted materials
nodes.publisher: Administrative Office of the U.S. Courts

nodes.label: ABA Model Rule 1.18: Duties to Prospective Client, Comment
nodes.limitation: The Model Rules are models and the existence and scope of duties are fact- and jurisdiction-dependent.
nodes.locator: Comments on limiting initial consultation and duties arising from prospective-client information

nodes.label: ABA Model Rule 5.3: Responsibilities Regarding Nonlawyer Assistance
nodes.limitation: The rule is a model and does not substitute for governing jurisdictional rules or a fact-specific vendor review.
nodes.locator: Managerial and supervisory duties for nonlawyer assistance

nodes.label: Arizona Alternative Business Structure Program
nodes.limitation: One jurisdiction's regulated program does not authorize a structure or practice in other jurisdictions and does not dissolve client duties.
nodes.locator: Program definition, licensing, regulation, and nonlawyer economic interest or decision authority

nodes.label: Palantir Ontology
nodes.limitation: Vendor product positioning supports the control-plane comparison; it does not prove custody, access, or behavior in any law-firm deployment.
nodes.locator: Ontology as enterprise data, logic, action, security, and Human+AI decision orchestration

safety_contract.authority: Authored registries and cited public sources; named human review is required for promotion or release.
